codeReview.summary: Fail. The workspace-root enforcement added for local sandbox reads, writes, and patch application appears to satisfy VAL-SANDBOX-005, but LocalDir staging still does not fully satisfy VAL-SANDBOX-004. The implementation only rejects symlinks on the exact path components it stats and its "source changed during copy" guard is just two back-to-back metadata checks before the actual copy, so a source reached through a symlinked ancestor or swapped after validation can still be ingested. The new regression test also does not exercise the claimed swapped-source case.

issues.description: `validate_local_dir_source_root()`/`stable_local_dir_entry_kind()` only call `symlink_metadata()` on the final path (`crates/agents-core/src/sandbox/mod.rs:755-788`), which does not reject LocalDir sources reached through a symlinked ancestor even though VAL-SANDBOX-004 explicitly requires that. In addition, `copy_local_dir_contents()` tries to detect mid-copy swaps by calling `stable_local_dir_entry_kind()` twice in a row before it recurses or calls `fs::copy` (`crates/agents-core/src/sandbox/mod.rs:721-742`). Because the source path is never pinned via an opened descriptor and is not revalidated after the copy starts, an attacker can still swap the source root or a child into a symlink/alternate tree after validation and before `read_dir`/`fs::copy`, allowing unsafe host content to be staged.

`localdir_staging_rejects_symlinked_or_swapped_sources()` only creates a pre-existing child symlink and then a stable success case (`crates/openai-agents/tests/sandbox_semantics.rs:263-317`). It never exercises a symlinked ancestor or any time-of-check/time-of-use swap during staging, so the regression suite does not actually cover the full VAL-SANDBOX-004 contract that the feature and test name claim to verify.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `rust-sandbox-worker` skill should call out adversarial sandbox-safety regressions for symlink ancestry and TOCTOU path swaps when a feature assertion is about workspace or LocalDir confinement. The current procedure was followed here, but it was still easy to treat a static-symlink regression as sufficient coverage for a contract that explicitly requires swapped-source rejection.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The skill only says to write failing deterministic tests and re-run targeted sandbox tests (`.factory/skills/rust-sandbox-worker/SKILL.md:25-27`), while the landed regression `crates/openai-agents/tests/sandbox_semantics.rs:263-317` is named for "symlinked or swapped sources" but only exercises a pre-existing child symlink.

summary: Reviewed the feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, `rust-sandbox-worker`, mission guidance, and commit `c4888860261b898616cd007803ce2605510c6e4d`. Status: fail. The rooted file/patch path changes look correct for VAL-SANDBOX-005, but LocalDir staging still misses VAL-SANDBOX-004 because ancestor-symlink and post-validation swap cases can evade the new checks, and the added regression does not cover those scenarios.
